core_mechanism: dynamic scaling near a critical point (critical slowing down): the characteristic relaxation/autocorrelation time diverges as a power law of the system size or correlation length, tau ~ L^z, with a single universal exponent z that is invariant across microscopically different (disordered) instances, defining one dynamic universality class
brief_reason: The abstract extracts a universal dynamic critical exponent z=2.35(2) from the size-dependence of autocorrelation times at criticality and finds the same value across several disordered models both in equilibrium and after a quench, which is exactly a power-law divergence of relaxation time governed by a universal dynamic-scaling exponent.
